input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- After install, run: `xcode-select --install` (command-line tools)
- Accept license: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`
- Open Xcode once to install any additional components
- **Discover version:** MAUI docs list the minimum Xcode version per .NET release at [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/what-is-maui](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/)

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest LTS or STS that supports MAUI
- Use the macOS **Arm64** installer if on Apple Silicon, **x64** for Intel
- The MAUI workload ships with the SDK but must be installed explicitly
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version`

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in iOS, Android, and platform-specific SDKs
- **Discover versions:** `dotnet workload search` lists available workloads; `dotnet workload list` shows what's installed
- **Update later:** `sudo dotnet workload update`

## 4. Install Android SDK Components

The MAUI workload installs a minimal Android SDK. You may still need:

- **Android Studio** (optional but recommended for emulator/AVD management): [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)
- Or manage via command line — the SDK is placed in `~/Library/Android/sdk` by the workload
- Accept Android licenses:
  ```bash
  dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies \
    -f net9.0-android \
    -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" \
    -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
  ```
- **Discover required API levels:** Check the MAUI release notes — they specify the target and minimum Android SDK versions.

## 5. Install JDK (Android requirement)

- The MAUI workload bundles a Microsoft OpenJDK build. If you need to install manually:
- **Source:** [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download)
- **Verify:** `java --version` (should be JDK 17+)

## 6. (Optional) Install an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> Visual Studio for Mac has been retired — VS Code or Rider are the current paths on macOS.

## 7. Verify the Full Toolchain

```bash
# Check SDK and workloads
dotnet --info
dotnet workload list

# Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>

# Run on Android Emulator (start one first via Android Studio or AVD)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

## 8. Discover the Right Versions

| What | How to check |
|------|-------------|
| Supported Xcode versions | [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) |
| .NET SDK compatibility | `dotnet --info` and [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) |
| Android API levels | `dotnet workload list` + [MAUI Android docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/android/) |
| Workload updates | `dotnet workload update --check` |

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → accept Android licenses → verify with `dotnet new maui` + build. Adjust the `net9.0-*` TFMs to match your installed SDK version.
